candidateContexts.label: admin basic-info CRUD facade over contacts/stations/trains/configs/prices
admin order CRUD facade routing to ts-order-service or ts-order-other-service by train number
admin route CRUD facade over ts-route-service with station membership pre-check
admin basic-info station CRUD proxy to ts-station-service
admin route station existence check against ts-station-service

candidateCovers.label: shared ts-station-service downstream boundary observed from admin basic-info CRUD and admin route existence check

notes: Spring Boot bootstrap class only, no business logic; declares a load-balanced RestTemplate bean used by the sibling service impl.
Five specific GET paths are opened before the blanket ADMIN role gate on the rest of the service prefix.
Every endpoint except welcome carries a method-level @CrossOrigin(origins="*") annotation, consistently applied across all five resource groups.
basicPriceRate and firstClassPriceRate are double rate multipliers, not absolute currency amounts; no money-amount cech observation applies to this DTO.
Pure interface declaration with javadoc; behavioral capability evidence lives in the implementing class instead.
Pure passthrough proxy to five downstream services (contacts, station, train, config, price); no local persistence or branching beyond building the target URL.
Registers with Nacos discovery on port 18767; no secrets or environment-specific values beyond the discovery address list.
Unlike admin-basic-info, no GET paths are opened before the blanket ADMIN role gate; the whole adminorderservice prefix requires ADMIN.
Only the GET endpoint carries @CrossOrigin(origins="*"); the three mutating endpoints have no per-method CORS annotation, unlike admin-basic-info's uniform coverage.
getAllOrders unconditionally merges results from both ts-order-service and ts-order-other-service; the other three methods branch to exactly one of the two by trainNumber prefix.
Port 16112, Nacos discovery, swagger controllerPackage points at adminorder.controller.
Same GET-only @CrossOrigin coverage pattern observed here as in admin-order-service's controller.
createAndModifyRoute pre-validates the start/end stations against the request's own station list before calling checkStationsExists against ts-station-service.
Only the bootstrap class fell in this chunk; the rest of ts-admin-travel-service (controller/service) is out of this chunk's worklist range.
